Decibel quantities add while ratio values multiply.
For linear systems, the frequency-domain input-output relation is Y(f) = X(f)H(f).
The output amplitude at a given frequency is found by adding the filter's gain in decibels to the input amplitude at that frequency.
The number of permutations of choosing k numbers from a pool of n is n (n − 1) (n − 2) ... (n − k + 1).
n! is defined as n (n − 1) (n − 2) ... 1.
0! is defined as 1.
The number of combinations is equal to the number of permutations divided by the number of orderings.
The number of ways to order a pool of k things is k!.
The symbol for the combination of k things drawn from a pool of n is  n choose k.
The combination of k things drawn from a pool of n equals n! / (k! (n − k)!).
The probability of any particular two-bit error sequence is p2(1 − p)n−2, where p is the probability of any bit being in error and n is the codeword length.
The sum of the probabilities of zero, one, two, etc. errors occurring in a codeword must equal one.
